quality.rationale: 标准坐标基底下的张量李导数推导，逻辑连贯且具泛化性，但属纯理论计算缺乏具体物理情境

origin.question:
Problem 8.13. The Lie derivative of a scalar function is defined to be the directional derivative: $\mathcal{L}_{\mathbf{x}}\mathbf{f} = 
abla_{\mathbf{x}}\mathbf{f}$ . For a vector field $\mathbf{y}$ , we define
Lie differentiation as $\mathcal{L}_{\mathbf{x}}\mathbf{y} \equiv [\mathbf{x},\mathbf{y}] = 
abla_{\mathbf{x}}\mathbf{y} - 
abla_{\mathbf{y}}\mathbf{x}$ . The Lie derivative obeys all the usual rules for a derivative operator and always gives a tensor of the same rank as the tensor differentiated.
(b) What is the Lie derivative of a tensor whose components are $\mathbf{T}^{\alpha} eta$ ?
origin.answer: $\\mathcal{L}_{x} T^{\\alpha}_{\\beta} = T^{\\alpha}_{\\beta, \\gamma} x^{\\gamma} - T^{\\mu}_{\\beta} x^{\\alpha}_{, \\mu} + T^{\\alpha}_{\\mu} x^{\\mu}_{, \\beta}$
origin.solution:
(b) For simplicity, work in a coordinate basis:
$$
\begin{array}{l}
\mathcal {L}_{\mathbf {X}} \mathbf {T} = \mathcal {L}_{\mathbf {X}} (\mathbf {T}^{a} \mathbf {\beta} \mathbf {e}_{a} \otimes \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\boldsymbol {\beta}}) \\
= \left(\mathcal {L}_{\mathbf {x}} \mathrm {T}^{\alpha} \beta\right) \mathbf {e}_{\alpha} \otimes \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\beta} + \mathrm {T}^{\alpha} \beta \left(\mathcal {L}_{\mathbf {x}} \mathbf {e}_{\alpha}\right) \otimes \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\beta} + \mathrm {T}^{\alpha} \beta \mathbf {e}_{\alpha} \otimes \left(\mathcal {L}_{\mathbf {x}} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\beta}\right) \\
= \mathrm {T}_{\beta , \gamma}^{\alpha} \mathrm {x}^{\gamma} \mathbf {e}_{\alpha} \otimes \tilde {\omega}^{\beta} - \mathrm {T}_{\beta}^{\alpha} \mathrm {x}^{\gamma},_{\alpha} \mathbf {e}_{\gamma} \otimes \tilde {\omega}^{\beta} + \mathrm {T}_{\beta}^{\alpha} \mathrm {x}^{\beta},_{\gamma} \mathbf {e}_{\alpha} \otimes \tilde {\omega}^{\gamma} \\
= \left(\mathrm {T}_{\beta , \gamma}^{\alpha} \mathbf {x}^{\gamma} - \mathrm {T}_{\beta , \mu}^{\mu} \mathbf {x}_{, \mu}^{\alpha} + \mathrm {T}_{\mu}^{\alpha} \mathbf {x}_{, \beta}^{\mu}\right) \mathbf {e}_{\alpha} \otimes \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\beta}. \\
\end{array}
$$
This is often written
$$
\mathcal {L}_{\mathbf {x}} \mathbf {T}^{\alpha}_{\beta} = \mathbf {T}_{\beta , \gamma}^{\alpha} \mathbf {x}^{\gamma} - \mathbf {T}_{\beta , \mu}^{\mu} \mathbf {x}_{, \mu}^{\alpha} + \mathbf {T}_{\mu}^{\alpha} \mathbf {x}_{, \beta}^{\mu}
$$
and the commas can be replaced by semicolons as in part (a).
